w:r.w:t: PRODUCT REQUIREMENTS DOCUMENT
ERPNext Banking Integration
EBICS Protocol — Full Feature Specification
Version: 1.0 | Date: April 2026 | Status: Draft for Review
Author: Spazorlabs Engineering | Platform: ERPNext (Frappe Framework)
1. Introduction
1.1 Purpose

This Product Requirements Document (PRD) defines the complete functional and non-functional requirements for the ERPNext Banking Integration module. The module integrates ERPNext with bank accounts via the EBICS (Electronic Banking Internet Communication Standard) protocol, enabling automated transaction import, intelligent payment matching, invoice reconciliation, SEPA outbound payment initiation, and automated dunning.
This document serves as the authoritative reference for engineering, QA, product, and stakeholder teams throughout the development lifecycle.
1.2 Product Overview
The Banking Integration module extends the existing ERPNext Frappe application with the following capabilities:
Secure bank account connectivity via OAuth / EBICS
Automated daily synchronisation of bank transactions
AI-assisted matching of transactions to invoices and payments with confidence scoring
Automated invoice status transitions (open → partially paid → paid)
Manual matching UI with suggestion ranking
Automated dunning (payment reminder) workflows
Outbound SEPA payment initiation (pain.001 XML) directly from purchase invoices
Full reconciliation loop including chargeback and failed payment handling
1.3 Scope

This PRD covers seven epics and twenty-five user stories as defined below. The module will be built as a custom Frappe app sitting atop the existing ERPNext instance. The existing Payments DocType will be extended rather than replaced.
1.4 Definitions & Abbreviations
Term
Definition
EBICS
Electronic Banking Internet Communication Standard — a protocol for secure bank-to-customer communication over the internet.
SEPA
Single Euro Payments Area — the EU payment integration initiative enabling EUR bank transfers.
pain.001
ISO 20022 XML message format for SEPA Credit Transfer initiation.
camt.053
ISO 20022 XML message format for bank-to-customer statement reporting.
Matching Engine
The algorithmic component that correlates bank transactions with ERPNext invoices and payments.
Confidence Score

A percentage (0–100%) indicating the system’s certainty that a transaction maps to a specific invoice/payment. Scores ≥90% trigger automatic assignment; scores below 90% are surfaced as suggestions for manual review.
DocType
A data model entity in the Frappe/ERPNext framework — analogous to a database table with a UI form.
Dunning
The process of sending escalating payment reminders to customers with overdue invoices.
OAuth
Open Authorisation — the redirect-based flow used to connect bank accounts.
IBAN
International Bank Account Number.
2. Goals & Objectives
2.1 Business Goals
Eliminate manual bank reconciliation work for accounting teams.
Reduce time-to-payment-confirmed from days to minutes via automatic matching.
Provide a single system of record linking bank transactions, payments, and invoices.
Enable outbound SEPA payments without leaving ERPNext, removing dependency on external banking portals.
Automate dunning to improve cash collection rates.
2.2 Success Metrics
Metric
Target
≥90% auto-match rate
90% or more of imported transactions matched automatically without human intervention.
Zero duplicate imports
Duplicate detection must prevent re-importing already-synced transactions.
pain.001 generation < 5 seconds
SEPA XML file generation completes within 5 seconds of user action.
Sync latency
Daily automatic sync completes within 15 minutes of scheduled trigger.
Chargeback detection
Negative transaction amounts flagged and payment reversal initiated within one sync cycle.
3. System Architecture
3.1 Architecture Overview

The system is composed of three logical zones: the ERPNext application layer, the EBICS integration service, and the external bank. Communication between ERPNext and the EBICS service is over HTTPS using signed and encrypted XML payloads conforming to EBICS 3.0.
Layer
Component
Responsibility
Presentation
Frontend (Frappe/ERPNext UI)
Banking connection wizard, transaction list, matching UI, suggestion panel, SEPA payment form.
Application
Backend API
Business logic orchestration, matching coordination, status transitions.
Application
Matching Engine
Scores transactions against invoices using amount, IBAN, and purpose/reference. Returns confidence score 0–100%.
Application
SEPA XML Generator
Produces ISO 20022 pain.001 XML files for outbound SEPA Credit Transfers.
Application
Job Scheduler
Triggers automatic transaction sync on a configurable schedule (default: daily).
Data
Payments DocType (extended)
Links Transaction ↔ Payment ↔ Invoice. Stores match confidence, status, and timestamps.
Data
Database
Persists transactions, bank accounts, matching state, and dunning records.
Integration
EBICS Service
Handles certificate exchange, transaction signing, and XML format mediation (camt.053 → internal, pain.001 → bank).
External
Bank EBICS Server
Processes signed HTTPS+XML requests. Provides camt.053 statements and accepts pain.001 payment files.
3.2 Data Flow Summary
Inbound (bank → ERPNext): Bank EBICS Server → EBICS Service (camt.053 parse) → Backend API → Matching Engine → Payments DocType → Invoice status update.
Outbound (ERPNext → bank): Purchase Invoice → Backend API → SEPA XML Generator (pain.001) → EBICS Service (sign & encrypt) → Bank EBICS Server.
3.3 Matching Confidence Logic
Score Range
Outcome
System Behaviour
Auto-assign
Transaction is automatically linked to the matched invoice. Invoice status updated. No user action required.
Suggestion
Match surfaced in the Suggestions panel with confidence score displayed. User performs one-click acceptance or manual override.
Unassigned
Transaction placed in the Open/Unassigned queue. User must manually search and assign.
N/A (negative amount)
Chargeback
Transaction flagged as chargeback. Linked payment reversed. Invoice re-opened.
4. Existing ERPNext Context
The integration builds upon the following existing ERPNext modules and DocTypes, visible in the provided application screenshots:
Existing Module
Relevance to This Project
Ausgangsrechnungen (Sales Invoices)
The primary DocType whose status field (Teilweise bezahlt / Bezahlt / Übfällig) will be driven by the matching engine. Auto-match updates invoice status automatically.
Eingangsrechnungen (Purchase Invoices)
Target DocType for SEPA outbound payments (Epic 7). The ‘Pay’ button will be added here. Statuses ‘In Payment’ and ‘Paid’ are managed by reconciliation.
Zahlungen (Payments)
Existing DocType extended (not replaced) to store the Transaction ↔ Payment ↔ Invoice triple-link, confidence score, match type, and timestamps.
Mahnungen (Dunning)
Existing dunning DocType whose deadline and fee configuration is consumed by the automated dunning workflow in Epic 6.
Buchhaltung (Accounting)
Parent module under which the new Banking submenu (bank accounts, transactions, matching) will be surfaced.
5. Epics & User Stories
The following sections specify each epic, its goals, and the full acceptance criteria for every user story. All items are required for the v1.0 delivery unless explicitly marked as out of scope.
Epic 1: Connect Bank Accounts
Goal: Allow users to securely connect and manage bank accounts within ERPNext.

This epic establishes the foundation of the integration. Users must be able to authenticate with their bank via a standard OAuth / redirect flow, select which accounts to track, manage multiple accounts, and renew credentials before expiry.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US1.1
As a user,
I want to connect my bank account so that transactions are automatically imported.
OAuth / redirect flow is implemented and functional.
Bank selection is available via the configured provider.
User can select which account(s) to connect.
Connection credentials are securely saved to the system.
Successful connection confirmation is displayed to the user.
US1.2
As a user,
I want to manage multiple bank accounts to ensure the current state is reflected in the system.
Multiple simultaneous bank accounts are supported.
Each account can be individually enabled, disabled, or deleted.
Account list displays IBAN and bank name for each entry.
Disabling an account pauses sync without deleting historical data.
US1.3
As a user,
I want to be able to renew my bank connection to ensure valid authentication at all times.
Connection expiry date/time is clearly visible on the account management screen.
Re-authentication flow is accessible from the account list.
System sends a reminder notification before connection expiry.
Expired connections are visually flagged and sync is paused until renewed.
Epic 2: Import Transactions
Goal: Establish reliable, automatic synchronisation of all bank account movements into ERPNext.
Once accounts are connected, all transaction data must flow into ERPNext reliably. This epic covers both the ongoing automated sync and the one-time historical import for newly connected accounts.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US2.1
As a user,
I want automatic synchronisation of my bank transactions so I can always view the latest movements.
Sync runs on a configurable schedule; default frequency is daily.
New transactions discovered since the last sync are saved to the database.
Duplicate detection prevents re-importing already-synced transactions (deduplication by transaction ID and date+amount+IBAN).
Sync status (last run time, transaction count) is visible to the user.
Sync can also be triggered manually on demand.
US2.2
As a user,
I want to import past transactions so I have a complete historical view from the start.
User can select a date range for the historical import.
Initial historical import is available as part of the first-time account setup wizard.
Historical import does not create duplicates if run multiple times.
Progress indicator shown during large imports.
US2.3
As a user,
I want to see all my bank transactions in a clear overview.
Transaction list displays: account holder, amount, date, purpose/reference, assignment status.
Status field shows one of: Assigned, Partially Assigned, Open.
List is filterable by account, date range, status, and amount.
List is sortable by date and amount.
Each transaction row links to the associated payment record (if matched).
Epic 3: Automatic Matching
Goal: Automatically assign incoming bank transactions to the correct invoices and payments.

The matching engine is the core intelligence of the module. It analyses each imported transaction against open invoices and payment records using multiple criteria, computes a confidence score, and acts accordingly. This epic also covers partial and bulk payment scenarios.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US3.1
As a user,
I want automatic assignment of my transactions to invoices so I do not have to match them manually.
Matching engine evaluates: amount (exact and tolerance-based), reference/purpose text, customer/supplier name.
Each candidate match receives a confidence score (0–100%).
Transactions with score ≥90% are automatically assigned without user intervention.
Transactions with score 50–89% are surfaced as suggestions (see US5.2).
Matching runs automatically after each sync cycle.
Matching logic is deterministic and auditable (confidence breakdown stored).
US3.2
As a user,
I want the system to detect partial payments so I can track outstanding balances accurately.
When a transaction amount is less than the invoice total, the invoice status is set to ‘Partially Paid’.
Remaining outstanding amount is calculated and displayed on the invoice.
Subsequent transactions for the same invoice are matched against the residual amount.
Full payment of remaining balance transitions invoice to ‘Paid’.
US3.3
As a user,
I want to assign bulk transfers so I can handle payments covering multiple invoices.
A single transaction can be split and assigned to multiple invoices.
UI is available for user-guided bulk splitting.
System suggests invoice candidates for each split portion.
Partial allocations are tracked; transaction is marked fully assigned only when 100% is allocated.
Epic 4: Invoice Status & Payment Records
Goal: Automatically maintain invoice statuses and create payment records upon successful matching.

This epic closes the loop between bank transactions and ERPNext’s accounting records. Every matched transaction must produce a corresponding payment entry and update the relevant invoice status without manual intervention.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US4.1
As a user,
I want the invoice status to update automatically when a matching payment is received.
Upon confirmed match, invoice status transitions to ‘Paid’ (or ‘Partially Paid’ for partial payments).
Transition timestamp is stored on the invoice record.
Status change is reflected immediately in the Ausgangsrechnungen (Sales Invoice) list view.
Status transitions are logged in the document activity feed for audit purposes.
US4.2
As a user,
I want automatic payment entries created in the system when a transaction is matched.
A Payment record is created for each matched transaction.
Payment record contains: transaction ID, amount, date, matched invoice reference, match confidence score.
The triple-link Transaction ↔ Payment ↔ Invoice is established and navigable.
The existing Payments DocType is extended (not replaced) to accommodate these fields.
Duplicate payment records are prevented.
US4.3
As a user,
I want to detect and handle chargebacks so that reversed payments are correctly reflected.
Incoming transactions with a negative amount are automatically classified as potential chargebacks.
On detection, the linked invoice is re-opened (status reverted to ‘Unpaid’ or ‘Overdue’).
The associated payment record is reversed/cancelled.
A chargeback event is logged with a timestamp and the original transaction reference.
The user receives an in-app notification of the detected chargeback.
Epic 5: Manual Post-Processing
Goal: Provide a clear, efficient UI for handling transactions that could not be automatically matched.

Not all transactions will reach the auto-match threshold. This epic provides the tools for accounting staff to efficiently handle the remainder: viewing suggestions, accepting them, or performing free-form manual assignment.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US5.1
As a user,
I want to manually assign transactions to invoices when automatic matching has not occurred.
User can open any unassigned transaction and search for an invoice to link it to.
Invoice search supports filter by customer, amount, date, and invoice ID.
Saving the manual assignment creates a Payment record and updates invoice status.
Manual assignments are flagged as ‘Manually Matched’ in the audit log.
US5.2
As a user,
I want to see system-generated matching suggestions so I can accept or override them quickly.
Suggestions panel displays all candidate matches with confidence score below 90%.
Each suggestion shows: invoice ID, customer, amount, due date, confidence score.
User can accept a suggestion with a single click.
User can reject a suggestion and perform a different manual assignment.
Accepted suggestions are promoted to confirmed matches and follow the same payment record creation flow as auto-matches.
US5.3
As a user,
I want a dedicated view of open / unassigned transactions so nothing is overlooked.
A filterable ‘Open Transactions’ view is available showing all unassigned transactions.
Filter options include: date range, account, amount range.
List is sortable by date (newest first by default) and amount.
Transaction count badge indicates the number of open items.
Bulk selection is supported for batch manual assignment.
Epic 6: Dunning (Automated Payment Reminders)
Goal: Automate the dunning process based on confirmed incoming payment data.

Dunning is the escalating sequence of payment reminders sent to customers with overdue invoices. This epic automates the trigger logic, using the banking integration data to determine whether a payment has been received before a dunning level is escalated.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US6.1
As a user,
I want to automate payment reminders so overdue invoices are chased without manual intervention.
Dunning check runs automatically after each sync cycle.
Deadlines, escalation levels, and fees are sourced from the existing ERPNext Dunning Types configuration.
A dunning step is only triggered if no matching payment has been received by the deadline.
Dunning documents are created in ERPNext and linked to the relevant invoice.
Dunning is suppressed for invoices already marked ‘Paid’ or ‘In Payment’.
The user can view the dunning status of any invoice from the invoice detail view.
Epic 7: SEPA Transfers — Outbound Payments from Purchase Invoices
Goal: Enable users to initiate SEPA payments directly from ERPNext purchase invoices.

This epic closes the payables loop. Users can trigger an outbound SEPA Credit Transfer for any purchase invoice directly from ERPNext. The system generates the compliant pain.001 XML, routes it through the EBICS service to the bank, and automatically reconciles the resulting bank debit against the invoice.
Story ID
User Story
Acceptance Criteria
US7.1
As a user,
I want to pay a purchase invoice directly from ERPNext without using an external banking portal.
A ‘Pay’ button is present on the purchase invoice detail view.
Payment form is pre-filled from supplier account data: IBAN, amount (outstanding balance), and reference.
All pre-filled fields are editable before submission.
On submission, a Payment record is created in ERPNext and the invoice status is set to ‘In Payment’.
A valid SEPA XML pain.001 file is generated and transmitted to the bank via the EBICS service.
User receives confirmation of successful submission.
US7.2
As a user,
I want to see a clear ‘In Payment’ status on purchase invoices so I know a SEPA transfer has been submitted.
Invoice status ‘In Payment’ is aligned with ERPNext’s standard payment status lifecycle.
Status is visible in both the invoice list view and the invoice detail view.
Hovering or clicking the status shows the associated payment record and submission timestamp.
US7.3
As a user,
I want automatic confirmation when my submitted SEPA payment has been executed by the bank.
Incoming camt.053 bank statement is parsed for debit entries matching outbound payments.
Match is performed against: amount, IBAN, and payment reference.
On successful match, invoice status transitions from ‘In Payment’ to ‘Paid’.
Reconciliation runs automatically on each sync cycle.
Manually triggered reconciliation is also supported.
US7.4
As a user,
I want to be notified when a SEPA payment has failed so I can take corrective action.
Failed payment status is parsed from bank return messages (EBICS status codes / camt.054 rejection).
Invoice status reverts from ‘In Payment’ to ‘Unpaid’.
A ‘Failed’ flag is set on the Payment record with the error code and reason.
The user receives an in-app notification and the failure is visible in the transaction and invoice views.
User can re-initiate the payment from the same invoice.
6. Non-Functional Requirements
Category
Requirement
Detail
Security
EBICS Compliance
All bank communication must use EBICS 3.0 with RSA-4096 certificate-based signing and AES-256 encryption. Credentials stored encrypted at rest.
Security
OAuth Token Storage
Bank OAuth tokens must be stored encrypted. Tokens must never be logged or exposed via API responses.
Security
Audit Trail
All matching decisions, status transitions, manual overrides, and payment submissions must be logged with user, timestamp, and before/after values.
Performance
Sync Latency
Daily automatic sync for up to 1,000 transactions must complete within 15 minutes.
Performance
Matching Throughput
Matching engine must process 1,000 transactions against up to 10,000 open invoices in under 60 seconds.
Performance
pain.001 Generation
SEPA XML generation must complete in under 5 seconds.
Reliability
Idempotency
All sync and import operations must be idempotent. Re-running a sync must not create duplicate records.
Reliability
Error Handling
All EBICS communication errors, XML parse failures, and matching engine exceptions must be caught, logged, and surfaced to the user with actionable messages.
Usability
Existing UI Conventions
All new screens must follow existing ERPNext/Frappe UI patterns (list view, form view, filter bar) to minimise user learning curve.
Maintainability
Configuration
Sync frequency, matching thresholds, and dunning check intervals must be configurable via ERPNext system settings without code changes.
Compatibility
ERPNext Version
Module must be compatible with the ERPNext version in use at the time of delivery. Major version upgrades are out of scope.
7. UI / UX Requirements
7.1 Navigation
All new views are accessible under the existing Buchhaltung (Accounting) module in the ERPNext sidebar, under a new ‘Banking’ submenu containing: Bank Accounts, Transactions, Matching & Suggestions.
7.2 Key Screens
Screen
Requirements
Bank Account List
List of connected accounts showing IBAN, bank name, connection status, expiry date, and last sync time. Actions: Connect New, Enable/Disable, Delete, Renew.
Transaction List
Paginated list of all imported transactions. Columns: date, account, counterparty, amount, reference, status badge. Filter bar with date, account, status, amount range. Sortable columns.
Matching / Suggestions Panel
Split view: left side shows unmatched transaction detail; right side shows ranked invoice candidates with confidence scores. Accept / Override / Skip actions per suggestion.
Open Transactions View
Filtered subset of Transaction List showing only status=Open. Bulk select + assign. Count badge in sidebar navigation.
SEPA Payment Form
Modal or slide-in panel on Purchase Invoice detail. Pre-filled fields: IBAN, amount, reference (all editable). Submit / Cancel actions. Status feedback inline.
Purchase Invoice List
Existing list extended with ‘In Payment’ status badge. No structural changes to existing columns.
Sales Invoice List
Existing list extended with ‘Partially Paid’ status badge (already shown in screenshots). Confidence score accessible via transaction link.
8. Data Model
8.1 New DocTypes
DocType
Key Fields
Notes
Bank Account
name, iban, bank_name, status (active/inactive), oauth_token (encrypted), expiry_date, last_sync
New DocType. Stores connection metadata per account.
Bank Transaction
name, bank_account, date, amount, currency, counterparty_name, counterparty_iban, reference, raw_data, status (open/assigned/partially_assigned)
New DocType. One record per imported transaction. Linked to Payment on match.
8.2 Extended DocTypes
DocType
Added Fields
Notes
Payment (existing)
bank_transaction (link), match_confidence (percent), match_type (auto/manual/suggestion), sepa_xml_reference, payment_status (in_payment/failed/paid)
Extended. Triple-link Transaction ↔ Payment ↔ Invoice established here.
Sales Invoice (existing)
(no new fields — status field already supports Partially Paid)
Existing status field values reused. Matching engine writes to existing status field.
Purchase Invoice (existing)
payment_status (in_payment/paid/failed)
Extended to support SEPA outbound payment lifecycle.
9. Integration Specifications
9.1 EBICS Protocol
Parameter
Specification
Protocol Version
EBICS 3.0
Transport
HTTPS (TLS 1.2 minimum)
Authentication
RSA-4096 subscriber key pair; certificate exchange during onboarding (out of scope for this delivery).
Encryption
AES-256 session key wrapped with bank’s public key.
Inbound format
camt.053 (bank-to-customer account statement) for transaction retrieval.
Outbound format
pain.001 (ISO 20022 SEPA Credit Transfer initiation) for outbound payments.
Error handling
EBICS technical return codes (EBICS_OK, EBICS_AUTHENTICATION_FAILED, etc.) must be parsed and mapped to user-facing error messages.
9.2 pain.001 XML Requirements
Generated XML must be valid against the ISO 20022 pain.001.001.03 schema.
Required fields: MsgId, CreDtTm, NbOfTxs, CtrlSum, InitgPty/Nm, PmtInf/PmtMtd (TRF), Dbtr/Nm, DbtrAcct/IBAN, CdtTrfTxInf/Amt/InstdAmt, CdtrAcct/IBAN, CdtrAgt/BIC, RmtInf/Ustrd.
MsgId must be unique per file; generated as ERPNext document name + timestamp.
File must be validated locally before transmission to the EBICS service.
9.3 camt.053 Parsing Requirements

All Ntry (entry) elements must be parsed for: amount (Amt), credit/debit indicator (CdtDbtInd), booking date (BookgDt), value date (ValDt), counterparty IBAN (RltdPties/CdtrAcct or DbtrAcct), counterparty name (RltdPties/Cdtr/Nm or Dbtr/Nm), remittance reference (RmtInf/Ustrd).
Duplicate detection is based on: bank transaction ID (AcctSvcrRef) + booking date + amount.
Negative Amt with CdtDbtInd = DBIT on a previously credited account triggers chargeback logic.
10. Out of Scope
The following items are explicitly excluded from this delivery and must not be assumed:
EBICS bank onboarding, subscriber key generation, and certificate exchange with the bank.
Production server deployment, CI/CD pipeline setup, or infrastructure provisioning.
End-user training, user manuals, or training video production.
Multi-currency support beyond EUR and the SEPA payment zone.
ERPNext platform licensing, hosting, or subscription costs.
Any user story, feature, or acceptance criterion not defined in this document.
Mobile application development or native mobile UI optimisation.
Integration with third-party accounting systems outside of ERPNext.
Custom reporting or business intelligence dashboards beyond the standard ERPNext report views.
11. Assumptions & Constraints
Item
Detail
EBICS Sandbox Access
Client will provide EBICS sandbox/test credentials and bank access before development of Epic 7 begins. Absence of this will delay Epic 7 delivery.
Interface Specification

A complete EBICS API interface description has not been received at time of writing. This PRD carries a ±10% hour-estimate tolerance pending receipt and review. Scope confirmation is conditional on this document.
ERPNext Version
This PRD is based on the ERPNext version visible in the provided screenshots. Compatibility with other versions is not guaranteed and is out of scope.
Payments DocType
The existing Payments DocType data model is sufficiently flexible to be extended. If structural constraints are discovered, a new linked DocType will be created instead; this will not affect scope.
Bank Provider
The EBICS service provider is selected and accessible. Integration with specific providers (e.g. Klarna Kosma, FinAPI) is provider-agnostic at the application layer.
Single Currency
All transactions are assumed to be EUR. Multi-currency logic is out of scope.
Development Environment
A working ERPNext development instance will be made available to the development team before sprint 1.
12. Document Revision History
Version
Date
Author
Changes
April 2026
Spazorlabs Engineering
